last_completed_item: D-227 — Explicit Windows target tier, minimum floor, and scalable-effects policy
current_item: D-228 — Windows distribution, installation, and update model
next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting the Windows distribution installation and update model for formal tests and eventual public delivery: distribute only portable zip archives with fully manual updates; require one self-updating launcher or storefront client; or use channel-specific packaging where internal and formal external tests may use signed or checksummed portable immutable artifacts while public release supports a primary storefront or installer with optional updates, explicit offline launch, standard per-user save locations, side-by-side or rollback-safe version handling, repair and uninstall behavior that never deletes saves, and a documented manual or DRM-free path if commercially practical. Preserve immutable promoted artifacts, current and previous known-good builds, versioned migrations with protected rollback, complete offline campaign and permanent guest play, Windows-first scope, privacy-safe local diagnostics, no mandatory account for campaign play, and future storefront integrations behind adapters. Include tester friction, antivirus and code-signing warnings, patch size, delta updates, failed updates, portable versus installed saves, multiple channels, ownership and DRM, clean uninstall, dependency installation, rollback, and avoiding a custom launcher before scale justifies it. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. D-221 through D-227 are checkpointed as queued but not formally batch-persisted; continue through D-230 unless the user changes the cadence or requests a handoff.
resume_instruction: Read assembly/context/NEW_CHAT_RESUME.md first and follow it exactly.
blockers: Recovered decisions D-001 through D-039 require re-verification before final requirements
The product repository uses a bootstrap kit installed before the latest handoff framework additions; current resume files were added directly for this test

The authoritative Intake Interviewer prompt path assembly/prompts/00-intake-interviewer.md is currently missing from this branch and should be restored from the framework before final intake generation
The D-101 through D-220 decisions are persisted as authoritative batch-extension artifacts and should be consolidated into the primary live log and session snapshot during a later maintenance pass

persistence.batching_rule: Checkpoint queued state after several questions for flow; formally persist D-221 through D-230 at D-230 unless an earlier handoff is requested

constraints: Ordinary branches run compilation fast deterministic edit-mode tests content validation generated-registry checks and tests targeted to affected systems
Before integration run relevant play-mode smoke tests and a Windows player smoke build covering launch menu input room load combat save resume and clean exit

Scheduled or milestone validation runs full regression representative performance and memory scenes save interruption recovery and migration checks clean-machine installation offline launch and accessibility settings persistence
Required integration checks retain identifiable artifacts and actionable diagnostics
Flaky tests are quarantined and repaired rather than repeatedly rerun until green
Android networking and complete co-op pipelines remain deferred until those workstreams begin

Each candidate artifact records unique build and content versions source commit Unity version dependency and generated-content fingerprints test reports symbols checksums and save-schema compatibility
The exact same artifact is promoted through developer internal external release-candidate and public channels
Any code content configuration or dependency change creates a new artifact rather than silently altering a tested build
Retain the current public or formal build the previous known-good build relevant migration tools and a save-safe rollback procedure
Formal external Stage 1 tests and Stage 2 milestones use identifiable immutable artifacts even if earlier prototypes use a lighter process

Local bounded logs record build content and save-schema versions session and major mission outcomes deaths restarts checkpoints banking loadout changes performance failures exceptions asset failures and save journal recovery results
Relevant input-device and settings state may be included without personal identifiers
Logs remain local by default and testers explicitly choose when to export or submit them
Formal test builds explain what is recorded and exclude or redact free-text names account data filenames and unrelated system information
Logs rotate automatically and a one-click support bundle combines diagnostics configuration build identity and optional tester notes
Remote telemetry remains a future opt-in consideration only if project scale justifies its privacy security hosting and retention costs
Every save records game content and schema versions migration history and stable content identifiers
Newer builds create immutable backups then validate and apply ordered idempotent tested migrations before atomically committing the result
Failed or interrupted migrations restore safely and older builds refuse to modify newer-schema saves
Rollback uses cloned saves or compatible backups rather than destructive downgrade
Removed content uses stable-ID tombstones replacements refunds or explicit compensation rules
Development channels may permit announced resets but archive or export old profiles first while public compatibility promises remain explicit and tested
Developer and approved internal builds expose deterministic room warps loadout setup enemy spawning state inspection performance overlays and fault injection
Formal external builds expose only commands required for the round through hidden or authenticated activation and log every use
Sessions altered by commands are excluded from ordinary fun achievement record and challenge evidence
Public builds exclude progression-altering commands and retain only safe support diagnostics through an explicit workflow
Save-changing diagnostics use temporary or cloned profiles rather than silently modifying the primary save
Practice accessibility level selection and future speedrun tools are legitimate player-facing systems rather than disguised cheats
Use one pinned Unity LTS editor version locked package versions reproducible configuration and an approved dependency inventory with license source version and purpose
Reject incompatible editor or package versions and disable automatic dependency upgrades
Every upgrade uses an isolated task and branch with compilation content build save performance smoke visual and asset-reimport validation plus rollback
Critical fixes may occur outside normal windows but must pass the same checks
Prefer small maintained dependencies wrapped behind project-owned adapters and avoid unnecessary broad frameworks
Do not introduce analytics storefront networking or mobile SDKs before those workstreams begin and retain metadata needed to reproduce promoted artifacts
The primary target sustains stable 60 FPS at 1080p during representative heavy combat with intended four-weapon enemy projectile lighting destruction and effect density
A declared minimum floor must complete the campaign readably using reduced visual settings without changing gameplay input saves collision simulation or progression
Higher-end settings add cosmetic quality resolution and refresh-rate headroom without gameplay advantage
Expose effect density shadows lighting debris shake flashes post-processing resolution display mode frame cap VSync and transparent presets
Validate on a small matrix covering the primary target minimum floor another GPU or driver family and clean Windows installations before major milestones
Track CPU GPU allocation memory loading atlas particle enemy and projectile budgets through stress scenes while real hardware remains authoritative
